test.observation: Orchestrator clarified: test_compressed_allreduce_working.py tests CANCELLED standalone kernel. test_compressed_fused_kernel.py produces NaN for BOTH compressed and uncompressed due to cross-WG synchronization limitations in Python threading. Full validation via C dispatch engine pipeline deferred to user-testing validator.

typecheck.observation: All Python files compile without syntax errors

lint.command: N/A - no lint tools (ruff/pylint/flake8) available in dev container
lint.observation: Lint tools not available in Docker environment

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add note to .factory/library/fused-gemv-patterns.md or compressed-allreduce.md about the cross-WG synchronization requirement for fused kernels: they cannot be tested via Python threading because all 4 GPUs must launch simultaneously with atomic counter coordination. Validation must go through C dispatch or end-to-end benchmarks.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Both test_compressed_fused_kernel.py and test_fused_gemv_isolate.py produce NaN for compressed AND uncompressed fused kernels when run via Python threading. The atomic gpu_done_counter requires all 4 GPUs to participate in the barrier.

findings.details: Each workgroup computes its own scale via max-abs reduction, quantizes FP16 to INT8. Buffer layout: [INT8 data (cols_per_gpu bytes)] [FP16 scales (num_wgs * 2 bytes)]
Reads INT8+scale from compressed_local for self GPU columns. Peer columns use correct peer_idx mapping: peer_idx = col_gpu - (col_gpu > tp_rank ? 1 : 0)
Quantization and dequantization use same layout: int8_data[col_in_gpu] and scale_data[col_in_gpu / COLS_PER_WG]
Maps GPU indices 0-3 to peer buffer indices 0-2 by skipping tp_rank
